action.reasoning: Observation: repeated attempts to reproduce the historical 0.8092 hidden score via retraining have plateaued around 0.79–0.792 despite decent local val, indicating substantial train/val-to-test variance and low confidence that more training tweaks will recover the lost checkpoint. Analysis: the highest expected balanced_accuracy now comes from recovering the original good model artifact rather than further optimization. The workspace still contains multiple checkpoints, and the iteration-6 best may survive under a different filename. Since predict.py can be adapted to read a manifest or choose a submodel, the cheapest high-value action is forensic inspection of all checkpoints’ metadata/state keys (especially infer_logit_tau, val metrics, resume lineage) and local val scores, then submit the most plausible surviving candidate if found. Decision: run a checkpoint audit script over all .pt files, evaluate each locally on val with predict.py-compatible inference, and if one stands out as matching the original calibrated decoupled model, submit it immediately; otherwise stop and preserve current best-known result.
